name: Operation
summary: The Operation class implements Tekla Structures level operations.
remarks: Using this method is much faster than checking each object individually.

methods.name: AddToPourUnit
methods.signature: public static bool AddToPourUnit(PourUnit inputPourUnit, List<ModelObject> objectsToBeAdded)
methods.summary: Adds model objects as part of a pour unit Model object types accepted are assembly types except cast in situ, reinforcements of different kind, components and bolts

params.name: inputPourUnit
params.doc: the instance of pour unit to add objects to.

params.name: objectsToBeAdded
params.doc: the list of model objects to be added.

returns.doc: True on success.

throws.when: Throw exceptions if Pour or object has invalid ID, do not exist in model or not valid object type.

methods.name: CalculatePourUnits
methods.signature: public static bool CalculatePourUnits()
methods.summary: Calculate and assign objects to pour unit Model object types that are associated with pour unit are assembly types except cast in situ, reinforcements of different kind, components and screws

returns.doc: True on success.

methods.name: Combine(Beam, Beam)
methods.signature: public static Beam Combine(Beam ObjectToCombineTo, Beam ObjectToBeCombined)
methods.summary: Combines two beams into one beam.

params.name: ObjectToCombineTo
params.doc: The beam to be combined to.

params.name: ObjectToBeCombined
params.doc: The beam which will be deleted after a successful operation.

returns.doc: The combined beam on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Combine(RebarGroup, RebarGroup)
methods.signature: public static RebarGroup Combine(RebarGroup ObjectToCombineTo, RebarGroup ObjectToBeCombined)
methods.summary: Combines two rebar groups into one rebar group.

params.name: ObjectToCombineTo
params.doc: The rebar group to be combined to.

params.name: ObjectToBeCombined
params.doc: The rebar group which will be deleted after a successful operation.

returns.doc: The combined rebar group on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Combine(SingleRebar, SingleRebar)
methods.signature: public static SingleRebar Combine(SingleRebar ObjectToCombineTo, SingleRebar ObjectToBeCombined)
methods.summary: Combines two single rebars into one rebar.

params.name: ObjectToCombineTo
params.doc: The rebar to be combined to.

params.name: ObjectToBeCombined
params.doc: The rebar which will be deleted after a successful operation.

returns.doc: The combined single rebar on success, null on failure.

methods.name: ConvertPartToItem
methods.signature: public static bool ConvertPartToItem(in Part originalPart, out Brep newItem)
methods.summary: Convert a part to an item. This will also create a new shape in the shape catalog.

params.name: originalPart
params.doc: Original part

params.name: newItem
params.doc: Newly created item

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

throws.when: Thrown if the originalPart is null.

methods.name: CopyObject(ModelObject, Vector)
methods.signature: public static ModelObject CopyObject(ModelObject Object, Vector CopyVector)
methods.summary: Copies the model object using the given translation vector.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The model object to copy.

params.name: CopyVector
params.doc: The translation vector for copying.

returns.doc: The copied model object on success, null on failure.

methods.name: CopyObject(ModelObject, CoordinateSystem, CoordinateSystem)
methods.signature: public static ModelObject CopyObject(ModelObject Object, CoordinateSystem StartCoordinateSystem, CoordinateSystem EndCoordinateSystem)
methods.summary: Copies the model object between the given translation coordinate systems.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The model object to copy.

params.name: StartCoordinateSystem
params.doc: The coordinate system to copy the object from.

params.name: EndCoordinateSystem
params.doc: The coordinate system to copy the object to.

returns.doc: The copied model object on success, null on failure.

methods.name: CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part, IList<Point>, Part, IList<Point>)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part part1, IList<Point> face1, Part part2, IList<Point> face2)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a cylindrical bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts and selected faces in each part. See CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part, IList<Point>, Part, IList<Point>, BentPlate.BendShape).

params.name: part1
params.doc: The first part.

params.name: face1
params.doc: The selected face on the first part.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The second part.

params.name: face2
params.doc: The selected face on the second part.

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

methods.name: CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part, Face, Part, Face)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part part1, Face face1, Part part2, Face face2)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a cylindrical bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts and selected faces in each part. See CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part, Face, Part, Face, BentPlate.BendShape).

params.name: part1
params.doc: The first part.

params.name: face1
params.doc: The selected face on the first part.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The second part.

params.name: face2
params.doc: The selected face on the second part.

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

methods.name: CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part, IList<Point>, Part, IList<Point>, Double)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part part1, IList<Point> face1, Part part2, IList<Point> face2, double radius)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts, selected faces in each part and radius. This method can change GUID when using from plug-ins. To keep GUID, use AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, Double).

params.name: part1
params.doc: The first part.

params.name: face1
params.doc: The selected face on the first part.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The second part.

params.name: face2
params.doc: The selected face on the second part.

params.name: radius
params.doc: The target radius for the created cylindrical section.

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

throws.when: Thrown if could not create ConnectiveGeometry instance.
Thrown when faces have incorrect number of face points or received unsupported bent plate creation input.

methods.name: CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part, IList<Point>, Part, IList<Point>, BentPlate.BendShape)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part part1, IList<Point> face1, Part part2, IList<Point> face2, BentPlate.BendShape bendShape)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts and selected faces in each part. This method can change GUID when using from plug-ins. To keep GUID, use AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, BentPlate.BendShape).

params.name: part1
params.doc: The first part.

params.name: face1
params.doc: The selected face on the first part.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The second part.

params.name: face2
params.doc: The selected face on the second part.

params.name: bendShape
params.doc: Shape of the bend (cylindrical or conical)

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

throws.when: Thrown if could not create ConnectiveGeometry instance.
Thrown when faces have incorrect number of face points or received unsupported bent plate creation input.

methods.name: CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part, Face, Part, Face, Double)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part part1, Face face1, Part part2, Face face2, double radius)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts and selected faces in each part and radius. This method can change GUID when using from plug-ins. To keep GUID, use AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, Double).

params.name: part1
params.doc: The first part.

params.name: face1
params.doc: The selected solid face on the first part.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The second part.

params.name: face2
params.doc: The selected solid face on the second part.

params.name: radius
params.doc: The target radius for the created cylindrical section.

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

throws.when: Thrown if could not create ConnectiveGeometry instance.
Thrown when faces have incorrect number of face points or received unsupported bent plate creation input.

methods.name: CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part, Face, Part, Face, BentPlate.BendShape)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateBentPlateByFaces(Part part1, Face face1, Part part2, Face face2, BentPlate.BendShape bendShape)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts and selected faces in each part. This method can change GUID when using from plug-ins. To keep GUID, use AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, BentPlate.BendShape).

params.name: part1
params.doc: The first part.

params.name: face1
params.doc: The selected solid face on the first part.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The second part.

params.name: face2
params.doc: The selected solid face on the second part.

params.name: bendShape
params.doc: Shape of the bend (cylindrical or conical)

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

throws.when: Thrown if could not create ConnectiveGeometry instance.
Thrown when faces have incorrect number of face points or received unsupported bent plate creation input.

methods.name: CreateBentPlateByParts(Part, Part)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateBentPlateByParts(Part part1, Part part2)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a cylindrical bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts. See CreateBentPlateByParts(Part, Part, BentPlate.BendShape).

params.name: part1
params.doc: One part used for creating the bent plate.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The other part used for creating the bent plate.

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

methods.name: CreateBentPlateByParts(Part, Part, Double)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateBentPlateByParts(Part part1, Part part2, double radius)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts and a radius. This method can change GUID when using from plug-ins. To keep GUID, use AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry, ConnectiveGeometry, Double).

params.name: part1
params.doc: One part used for creating the bent plate.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The other part used for creating the bent plate.

params.name: radius
params.doc: The target radius for the created cylindrical section.

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise

throws.when: Thrown if could not create ConnectiveGeometry instance.
Thrown if received unsupported bent plate creation input.

methods.name: CreateBentPlateByParts(Part, Part, BentPlate.BendShape)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateBentPlateByParts(Part part1, Part part2, BentPlate.BendShape bendShape)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts. This method can change GUID when using from plug-ins. To keep GUID, use AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry, ConnectiveGeometry, BentPlate.BendShape).

params.name: part1
params.doc: One part used for creating the bent plate.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The other part used for creating the bent plate.

params.name: bendShape
params.doc: Shape of the bend (cylindrical or conical)

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

throws.when: Thrown if could not create ConnectiveGeometry instance.
Thrown if received unsupported bent plate creation input.

methods.name: CreateConicalBentPlateByFaces(Part, IList<Point>, Part, IList<Point>, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateConicalBentPlateByFaces(Part part1, IList<Point> face1, Part part2, IList<Point> face2, double largestRadius, double halfAperture)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a conical bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts, selected faces in each part and radius. This method can change GUID when using from plug-ins. To keep GUID, use AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, Double).

params.name: part1
params.doc: The first part.

params.name: face1
params.doc: The selected face on the first part.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The second part.

params.name: face2
params.doc: The selected face on the second part.

params.name: largestRadius
params.doc: The largest target radius for the created conical section.

params.name: halfAperture
params.doc: Angle between a generatrix of the cone and its center line (i.e. axis)

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

throws.when: Thrown if could not create ConnectiveGeometry instance.
Thrown when faces have incorrect number of face points or received unsupported bent plate creation input.

methods.name: CreateConicalBentPlateByFaces(Part, Face, Part, Face, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateConicalBentPlateByFaces(Part part1, Face face1, Part part2, Face face2, double largestRadius, double halfAperture)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a conical bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts and selected faces in each part, and the largest radius of the conical section and the cone aperture. This method can change GUID when using from plug-ins. To keep GUID, use AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, ConnectiveGeometry, LineSegment, Double).

params.name: part1
params.doc: The first part.

params.name: face1
params.doc: The selected solid face on the first part.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The second part.

params.name: face2
params.doc: The selected solid face on the second part.

params.name: largestRadius
params.doc: Largest radius of the conical section

params.name: halfAperture
params.doc: Angle between a generatrix of the cone and its center line (i.e. axis)

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise.

throws.when: Thrown if could not create ConnectiveGeometry instance.
Thrown when faces have incorrect number of face points or received unsupported bent plate creation input.

methods.name: CreateConicalBentPlateByPartsAndAperture
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateConicalBentPlateByPartsAndAperture(Part part1, Part part2, double largestRadius, double halfAperture)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a conical bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts. The resulting bend will have the given aperture and the provided larger radius.

params.name: part1
params.doc: One part used for creating the bent plate.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The other part used for creating the bent plate.

params.name: largestRadius
params.doc: Radius of the largest section of the cone

params.name: halfAperture
params.doc: Angle between a generatrix of the cone and its center line (i.e. axis)

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise

methods.name: CreateConicalBentPlateByPartsAndTwoRadiuses
methods.signature: public static BentPlate CreateConicalBentPlateByPartsAndTwoRadiuses(Part part1, Part part2, double firstRadius, double secondRadius)
methods.summary: Modifies the first plate by adding a conical bend that connects it to the second plate creating a new BentPlate instance based on two parts. The resulting bend will have the two given radiuses.

params.name: part1
params.doc: One part used for creating the bent plate.

params.name: part2
params.doc: The other part used for creating the bent plate.

params.name: firstRadius
params.doc: Radius of one section of the cone

params.name: secondRadius
params.doc: Radius of the other section of the cone

returns.doc: The bent plate object if successful, null otherwise

methods.name: CreateDGNv8Export
methods.signature: public static bool CreateDGNv8Export(string FileName, string Folder, Operation.ExportBasePoint BasePoint, string BasePointGuid, string ObjectColoring, string ExportLayersAs, bool OnlyFromSelectedObjects)
methods.summary: Dgn export from all or selected objects.

params.name: FileName
params.doc: The file name.

params.name: Folder
params.doc: The folder.

params.name: BasePoint
params.doc: Defines which base point is used in export.

params.name: BasePointGuid
params.doc: Base point guid if BasePoint parameter is set to BASE_POINT.

params.name: ObjectColoring
params.doc: Defines which object coloring filter is to be used in export.

params.name: ExportLayersAs
params.doc: Defines which report field to use for layers export.

params.name: OnlyFromSelectedObjects
params.doc: Defines if only selected (value true) or all (value false) objects are exported.

returns.doc: True, if Dgn export was successful, otherwise false.

methods.name: CreateIFC4ExportFromAll
methods.signature: public static bool CreateIFC4ExportFromAll(string fullPathAndFileName, Operation.IFCExportViewTypeEnum exportViewType, IEnumerable<string> propertySets, Operation.ExportBasePoint locationByValue, string exportLayersAsValue, string objectColoring, Operation.IFCExportFlags flags, string basePointGuid)
methods.summary: Creates IFC4 export from all objects using the given file name. See Tekla Structures Help for more information about IFC4 export files.

params.name: fullPathAndFileName
params.doc: The full path and file name including extension (.ifc or .ifcZip).

params.name: exportViewType
params.doc: The export view type.

params.name: propertySets
params.doc: The full path and file name of the property set files (.xml) to use in export.

params.name: locationByValue
params.doc: The location by value.

params.name: exportLayersAsValue
params.doc: The export layers as value: __Name__, __Phase__ or UDA value.

params.name: objectColoring
params.doc: The object coloring value: Use "ByObjectClass" for color by object class. For additional coloring, use the name of the object representation.

params.name: flags
params.doc: The additional flags.

params.name: basePointGuid
params.doc: Base point guid, if a base point used.

returns.doc: True, if Ifc4 export was successful, otherwise false. See session log for additional info in case failure.

methods.name: CreateIFC4ExportFromSelected
methods.signature: public static bool CreateIFC4ExportFromSelected(string fullPathAndFileName, Operation.IFCExportViewTypeEnum exportViewType, IEnumerable<string> propertySets, Operation.ExportBasePoint locationByValue, string exportLayersAsValue, string objectColoring, Operation.IFCExportFlags flags, string basePointGuid)
methods.summary: Creates IFC4 export from the selected parts using the given file name. See Tekla Structures Help for more information about IFC4 export files.

params.name: fullPathAndFileName
params.doc: The full path and file name including extension (.ifc or .ifcZip).

params.name: exportViewType
params.doc: The export view type.

params.name: propertySets
params.doc: The full path and file name of the property set files (.xml) to use in export.

params.name: locationByValue
params.doc: The location by value.

params.name: exportLayersAsValue
params.doc: The export layers as value: __Name__, __Phase__ or UDA value.

params.name: objectColoring
params.doc: The object coloring value: Use "ByObjectClass" for color by object class. For additional coloring, use the name of the object representation.

params.name: flags
params.doc: The additional flags.

params.name: basePointGuid
params.doc: Base point guid, if a base point used.

returns.doc: True, if Ifc4 export was successful, otherwise false. See session log for additional info in case failure.

methods.name: CreateMISFileFromAll
methods.signature: public static bool CreateMISFileFromAll(Operation.MISExportTypeEnum MISType, string FileName)
methods.summary: Creates MIS files from all parts using the given file name. See Tekla Structures Help for more information about MIS files.

params.name: MISType
params.doc: The type of the MIS export.

params.name: FileName
params.doc: The name of the MIS file to be used in creation.

returns.doc: True if the MIS files are created, false if the numbering is not up-to-date or the used configuration is wrong.

throws.when: Thrown when the FileName is null.
Thrown when the FileName is not defined.

methods.name: CreateMISFileFromSelected
methods.signature: public static bool CreateMISFileFromSelected(Operation.MISExportTypeEnum MISType, string FileName)
methods.summary: Creates MIS files from the selected parts using the given file name. See Tekla Structures Help for more information about MIS files.

params.name: MISType
params.doc: The type of the MIS export.

params.name: FileName
params.doc: The name of the MIS file to be used in creation.

returns.doc: True if the MIS files are created, false if the numbering is not up-to-date or the used configuration is wrong.

throws.when: Thrown when the FileName is null.
Thrown when the FileName is not defined.

methods.name: CreateNCFilesByPartId
methods.signature: public static bool CreateNCFilesByPartId(string NCFileSettings, string DestinationFolder, Identifier PartID, out string DstvOutput, bool CreatePopMarks = false, string PopMarkSettingsFileName = "", bool CreateContourMarking = false, string ContourMarkingSettingsFileName = "")
methods.summary: Creates NC files from the selected parts using the given NC template name. See Tekla Structures Help for more information about NC files.

params.name: NCFileSettings
params.doc: The name of the NC setting template to be used in creation.

params.name: DestinationFolder
params.doc: The name of the folder where NC files are created. If defined, overrides the default folder in the setting template.

params.name: PartID
params.doc: The identifier of the part.

params.name: DstvOutput
params.doc: The DSTV output as string.

params.name: CreatePopMarks
params.doc: Create pop-marks during export.

params.name: PopMarkSettingsFileName
params.doc: The name of the pop-mark setting file to be used in creation.

params.name: CreateContourMarking
params.doc: Create contour marking during export.

params.name: ContourMarkingSettingsFileName
params.doc: The name of the contour marking setting file to be used in creation.

returns.doc: True if the NC files are created, false if the numbering is not up-to-date or the used configuration is wrong.

throws.when: Thrown when the NCFileSettings is not defined.
Thrown when the NCFileSettings is null.

methods.signature: public static bool CreateNCFilesFromSelected(string NCFileSettings, string DestinationFolder, bool CreatePopMarks = false, string PopMarkSettingsFileName = "", bool CreateContourMarking = false, string ContourMarkingSettingsFileName = "")
methods.summary: Creates NC files from the selected parts using the given NC template name. See Tekla Structures Help for more information about NC files.

params.doc: The name of the NC setting template to be used in creation.

params.doc: The name of the folder where NC files are created. If defined, overrides the default folder in the setting template.

methods.name: CreateReportFromAll
methods.signature: public static bool CreateReportFromAll(string TemplateName, string FileName, string Title1, string Title2, string Title3)
methods.summary: Creates a report from all the objects using the given template name and filename. A template with the name given in the TemplateName parameter must exist in model folder or in the folder defined with the advanced option XS_TEMPLATE_DIRECTORY.If a path is not given in the filename, the file is created to the folder defined with the advanced option XS_REPORT_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY.If the given folder does not exist, the report creation fails.Internally, this method is asynchronous, and because of that the output file cannot be immediately available.See Tekla Structures Help for more information about reports.

params.name: TemplateName
params.doc: The name of the report template to be used in report creation. The name must contain more than three characters.

params.name: FileName
params.doc: The name of the created report. The name must contain more than three characters.

params.name: Title1
params.doc: The first title for the created report.

params.name: Title2
params.doc: The second title for the created report.

params.name: Title3
params.doc: The third title for the created report.

returns.doc: True if the report is created.

throws.when: Thrown when the TemplateName or FileName is null.
Thrown when the TemplateName or FileName is too short.

methods.name: CreateReportFromSelected
methods.signature: public static bool CreateReportFromSelected(string TemplateName, string FileName, string Title1, string Title2, string Title3)
methods.summary: Creates a report from the selected objects using the given template name and filename. A template with the name given in the TemplateName parameter must exist in model folder or in the folder defined with the advanced option XS_TEMPLATE_DIRECTORY.If a path is not given in the filename, the file is created to the folder defined with the advanced option XS_REPORT_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY.If the given folder does not exist, the report creation fails.See Tekla Structures Help for more information about reports.

params.name: TemplateName
params.doc: The name of the report template to be used in report creation. The name must contain more than three characters.

params.name: FileName
params.doc: The name of the created report. The name must contain more than three characters.

params.name: Title1
params.doc: The first title for the created report.

params.name: Title2
params.doc: The second title for the created report.

params.name: Title3
params.doc: The third title for the created report.

returns.doc: True if the report is created.

throws.when: Thrown when the TemplateName or FileName is null.
Thrown when the TemplateName or FileName is too short.

methods.name: CreateShapeFromGeometry
methods.signature: public static string CreateShapeFromGeometry(Part originalPart)
methods.summary: Create a new shape in the shape catalog, based on the part geometry.

params.name: originalPart
params.doc: Original part

returns.doc: The newly created shape name, or empty string on failure

throws.when: Thrown if the originalPart is null.

methods.name: DisplayPrompt
methods.signature: public static bool DisplayPrompt(string Message)
methods.summary: Displays a message in the status bar.
methods.remarks: Prompts Tekla Structures prefixes the given prompt with "prompt_" and looks for a translation in the prompts.ail file. If the translation (e.g. "prompt_Pick_first_position") is not found in the prompts.ail file, the prompt string is displayed as such. This feature can be used to give already translated strings to the picker.

params.name: Message
params.doc: The message to display.

returns.doc: True if the message could be displayed.

throws.when: Thrown when the Message is null.

methods.name: DisplayReport
methods.signature: public static bool DisplayReport(string FileName)
methods.summary: Opens and displays a report with the given name. If a path is not given in the filename, the file is searched from the folder defined with the advanced option XS_REPORT_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY.See Tekla Structures Help for more information about reports.

params.name: FileName
params.doc: The name of the report to display. The name must contain more than three characters.

returns.doc: True if the report existed.

throws.when: Thrown when the file specified in the FileName is not found or when the FileName is too short.

methods.name: ExplodeBentPlate
methods.signature: public static bool ExplodeBentPlate(BentPlate bentPlate)
methods.summary: Deletes bentPlate and inserts ContourPlates instances equivalent to the ones used to create bentPlate. The ContourPlate created from the main polygon has the same identifier as bentPlate.

params.name: bentPlate
params.doc: the BentPlate instance to explode.

returns.doc: True on success.

throws.when: Thrown when bentPlate is invalid.

methods.name: GetHandlePoints
methods.signature: public static List<Point> GetHandlePoints(string guid)
methods.summary: Get the Shape Item Handle Points

params.name: guid
params.doc: The guid of the shape

returns.doc: List of Handle Points

methods.name: GetSimilarNumberedObjects
methods.signature: public static List<ModelObject> GetSimilarNumberedObjects(ModelObject ObjectToCompare)
methods.summary: Gets similar objects based on numbering of given object.
methods.remarks: This method works currently only with parts and assemblies.

params.name: ObjectToCompare
params.doc: The object for comparison.

returns.doc: List of similar objects.

methods.name: Group
methods.signature: public static RebarGroup Group(IEnumerable RebarList)
methods.summary: Groups a list of single rebars or rebar groups and creates a new rebar group.

params.name: RebarList
params.doc: The list of single rebars and rebar groups to be grouped.

returns.doc: The created rebar group on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Highlight
methods.signature: public static bool Highlight(List<ModelObject> ModelObjects)
methods.summary: Highlight the list of objects from the user interface. Only the list of objects will be highlighted, the rest will be unhighlighted. But it will keep the status of the selected objects. To unhighlight give an empty list as a parameter.

params.name: ModelObjects
params.doc: The list of model objects to highlight.

returns.doc: True on success.

methods.name: IsMacroRunning
methods.signature: public static bool IsMacroRunning()
methods.summary: Returns true if a macro is running, false otherwise. Macros are saved as *.cs files in the folder defined with the XS_MACRO_DIRECTORY variable.See Tekla Structures Help for more information about macros.

returns.doc: True if a macro is running.

methods.name: IsModelAutoSaved
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use the method in ModelHandler class instead. Will be removed in near future.")] public static bool IsModelAutoSaved(string ModelFolder)
methods.summary: Tells whether a model has auto saved information.

params.name: ModelFolder
params.doc: The model folder to be used.

returns.doc: True if there is auto saved information.

methods.name: IsNumberingAllowed
methods.signature: public static bool IsNumberingAllowed()
methods.summary: Checks whether the numbering is allowed or not in the current configuration.

returns.doc: True if the numbering is allowed.

methods.name: IsNumberingUpToDate
methods.signature: public static bool IsNumberingUpToDate(ModelObject InputModelObject)
methods.summary: Checks whether the numbering is up-to-date for an assembly, part, rebar, surface treatment, pour object or break.

params.name: InputModelObject
params.doc: The model object to check. The object must be an assembly, a part, a rebar or an inherited object.

returns.doc: True if the numbering information is up-to-date.

throws.when: Thrown if the InputModelObject is not of a correct type or if it is not valid.

methods.name: IsNumberingUpToDateAll
methods.signature: public static bool IsNumberingUpToDateAll()
methods.summary: Checks whether the numbering is up-to-date for every assembly, part and rebar on the model. Remarks Using this method is much faster than checking each object individually.
methods.remarks: Using this method is much faster than checking each object individually.

returns.doc: True if the numbering information is up-to-date.

methods.name: MoveObject(ModelObject, Vector)
methods.signature: public static bool MoveObject(ModelObject Object, Vector TranslationVector)
methods.summary: Moves the model object using the given translation vector.
methods.remarks: Note that the object is moved and updated in the view so ModelObject.Modify() is not needed. Call Modify() only after the object's data has been updated with the ModelObject.Select() method.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The model object to move.

params.name: TranslationVector
params.doc: The vector for moving the object.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: MoveObject(ModelObject, CoordinateSystem, CoordinateSystem)
methods.signature: public static bool MoveObject(ModelObject Object, CoordinateSystem StartCoordinateSystem, CoordinateSystem EndCoordinateSystem)
methods.summary: Moves the model object between the given translation coordinate systems.
methods.remarks: Note that the object is moved and updated in the view so ModelObject.Modify() is not needed. Call Modify() only after the object's data has been updated with the ModelObject.Select() method.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The model object to move.

params.name: StartCoordinateSystem
params.doc: The coordinate system to move the object from.

params.name: EndCoordinateSystem
params.doc: The coordinate system to move the object to.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: ObjectMatchesToFilter(ModelObject, String)
methods.signature: public static bool ObjectMatchesToFilter(ModelObject ModelObject, string FilterName)
methods.summary: Checks whether the object matches to the criteria in the given filter.

params.name: ModelObject
params.doc: The model object to check.

params.name: FilterName
params.doc: The filter file to check against.

returns.doc: True if the object matches to the given filter criteria.

throws.when: Thrown when the ModelObject is not valid.

methods.name: ObjectMatchesToFilter(ModelObject, FilterExpression)
methods.signature: public static bool ObjectMatchesToFilter(ModelObject ModelObject, FilterExpression FilterExpression)
methods.summary: Checks whether the object matches to the criteria in the given filter.

params.name: ModelObject
params.doc: The model object to check.

params.name: FilterExpression
params.doc: The definition of a selection filter to check against.

returns.doc: True if the object matches to the given filter criteria.

throws.when: Thrown when the ModelObject is not valid.

methods.name: Open(String)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use the method in ModelHandler class instead. Will be removed in near future.")] public static bool Open(string ModelFolder)
methods.summary: Opens a new model to Tekla Structures ignoring auto saved information.

params.name: ModelFolder
params.doc: The model folder to be used.

returns.doc: True on success.

methods.name: Open(String, Boolean)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use the method in ModelHandler class instead. Will be removed in near future.")] public static bool Open(string ModelFolder, bool OpenAutoSaved)
methods.summary: Opens a new model to Tekla Structures.

params.name: ModelFolder
params.doc: The model folder to be used.

params.name: OpenAutoSaved
params.doc: Tells whether to open auto saved information or not.

returns.doc: True on success.

methods.name: RemoveFromPourUnit
methods.signature: public static bool RemoveFromPourUnit(List<ModelObject> objectsToBeRemoved)
methods.summary: Removes model object from pour unit Model object types accepted are assembly types except cast in situ, reinforcements of different kind, components and bolts

params.name: objectsToBeRemoved
params.doc: the list of model objects to be added.

returns.doc: True on success.

throws.when: Throw exceptions if object has invalid ID, do not exist in model or not valid object type.

methods.name: RunMacro
methods.signature: public static bool RunMacro(string FileName)
methods.summary: Starts a macro with the given name. Throws an exception if the file is not found. Macros are saved as *.cs files in the folder defined with the XS_MACRO_DIRECTORY variable.It is possible to run drawing macros using relative paths.See Tekla Structures Help for more information about macros.

params.name: FileName
params.doc: The name of the macro to start.

returns.doc: True if the macro existed.

methods.name: SaveAsWebModel
methods.signature: public static bool SaveAsWebModel(string Filename)
methods.summary: Saves the current model as a web model. You can save the model as a web model that can be viewed via the Internet using a web browser (e.g. Internet Explorer).

params.name: Filename
params.doc: The filename to be used.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SaveSelectedAsWebModel
methods.signature: public static bool SaveSelectedAsWebModel(string Filename)
methods.summary: Saves the selected objects as a web model. You can save the selected objects as a web model that can be viewed via the Internet using a web browser (e.g. Internet Explorer).

params.name: Filename
params.doc: The filename to be used.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetHandlePoints
methods.signature: public static bool SetHandlePoints(string guid, List<Point> HandlePoints)
methods.summary: Set the Shape Item Handle Points

params.name: guid
params.doc: The guid of the shape

params.name: HandlePoints
params.doc: An array of handle points

returns.doc: True if the appending operation was successful

methods.name: ShowOnlySelected
methods.signature: public static void ShowOnlySelected(Operation.UnselectedModeEnum UnselectedMode)
methods.summary: Show Only Selected objects in current view.

params.name: UnselectedMode
params.doc: Specify what to do with unselected parts.

methods.name: Split(Beam, Point)
methods.signature: public static Beam Split(Beam Object, Point SplitPoint)
methods.summary: Splits the beam and creates a new one in the given position.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The beam object to be split.

params.name: SplitPoint
params.doc: The position where splitting is executed.

returns.doc: The created beam on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Split(CircleRebarGroup, Line)
methods.signature: public static CircleRebarGroup Split(CircleRebarGroup Object, Line SplitLine)
methods.summary: Splits the circle rebar group and creates a new one in the given position.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The circle rebar group object to be splitted.

params.name: SplitLine
params.doc: The line where splitting is executed.

returns.doc: The created circle rebar group on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Split(ContourPlate, Polygon)
methods.signature: public static ContourPlate Split(ContourPlate Object, Polygon SplitPolygon)
methods.summary: Splits the contour plate and creates a new one along the given polygon.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The contour plate object to be splitted.

params.name: SplitPolygon
params.doc: The position where splitting is executed.

returns.doc: The created contour plate on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Split(CurvedRebarGroup, Line)
methods.signature: public static CurvedRebarGroup Split(CurvedRebarGroup Object, Line SplitLine)
methods.summary: Splits the curved rebar group and creates a new one in the given position.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The curved rebar group object to be splitted.

params.name: SplitLine
params.doc: The line where splitting is executed.

returns.doc: The created curved rebar group on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Split(LoftedPlate, Point)
methods.signature: public static LoftedPlate Split(LoftedPlate Object, Point SplitPoint)
methods.summary: Splits the lofted plate and creates a new one in the given position.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The lofted plate to be split.

params.name: SplitPoint
params.doc: The position where splitting is executed.

returns.doc: The created lofted plate on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Split(PolyBeam, Point)
methods.signature: public static PolyBeam Split(PolyBeam Object, Point SplitPoint)
methods.summary: Splits the polybeam and creates a new one in the given position.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The beam object to be split.

params.name: SplitPoint
params.doc: The position where splitting is executed.

returns.doc: The created polybeam on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Split(RebarGroup, Line)
methods.signature: public static RebarGroup Split(RebarGroup Object, Line SplitLine)
methods.summary: Splits the rebar group and creates a new one in the given position.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The rebar group object to be splitted.

params.name: SplitLine
params.doc: The line where splitting is executed.

returns.doc: The created rebar group on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Split(SingleRebar, Line)
methods.signature: public static SingleRebar Split(SingleRebar Object, Line SplitLine)
methods.summary: Splits the single rebar and creates a new one in the given position.

params.name: Object
params.doc: The single rebar object to be splitted.

params.name: SplitLine
params.doc: The line where splitting is executed.

returns.doc: The created single rebar on success, null on failure.

methods.name: SplitSlab
methods.signature: public static bool SplitSlab(int PartId, FacetedBrep Polymesh)
methods.summary: This command is meant for specifically splitting a concrete slab with advanced solid operations to create more robust and user friendly results than the command: public static ContourPlate Split(ContourPlate Object, Polygon SplitPolygon). No validation is done for the type, it is the caller's responsibility to call this only for valid types (slabs). Behavior for non-slabs is undetermined.

params.name: PartId
params.doc: The part ID that identifies the slab to be split

params.name: Polymesh
params.doc: The polymesh that defines the splitting surface

returns.doc: True on success.

methods.name: Ungrouping(RebarGroup)
methods.signature: public static ModelObjectEnumerator Ungrouping(RebarGroup Reinforcement)
methods.summary: Ungroups the rebar group and creates new single rebars.

params.name: Reinforcement
params.doc: The rebar group to be ungrouped.

returns.doc: An enumerator of single rebars.

methods.name: Ungrouping(RebarMesh)
methods.signature: public static ModelObjectEnumerator Ungrouping(RebarMesh Reinforcement)
methods.summary: Ungroups the rebar mesh and creates new single rebars.

params.name: Reinforcement
params.doc: The rebar mesh to be ungrouped.

returns.doc: An enumerator of single rebars.
